overall.description: A first-person walk through a historic district at night, starting along a covered colonnade past a theater entrance, then crossing a cobblestone town square as rain begins to fall. The footage is from a high-quality video game (Red Dead Redemption 2), featuring detailed 19th-century architecture and atmospheric gas lighting.

segments.scene_description: The segment begins on a dark street lined with stone walls and iron fences, entering a covered colonnade with massive stone pillars. Warm gas lamps illuminate the cobblestone path as a pedestrian passes by, and historic buildings with glowing windows line the route.

segments.scene_description: Walking through the covered colonnade, the camera approaches a brightly lit theater entrance. The stone pillars now line the left side while the right wall displays framed theater posters. The warm glow of marquee lights draws the eye toward the exit ahead.

scene_state.functional_zone: theater entrance and open cobblestone town square

segments.scene_description: Exiting the covered walkway, the scene opens into a spacious cobblestone town square. The Théâtre Râleur's ornate marquee dominates the right side with its bright lights and vertical sign. A horse rider crosses the square as pedestrians stroll through the warm glow of street lamps under the night sky.

scene_state.functional_zone: rainy town square with bandstand gazebo

agents.ego_description: walking across the open square toward the gazebo as rain begins to fall

low stone wall with decorative pillars lining the square

segments.scene_description: Walking deeper into the town square, rain begins to fall, creating glistening reflections on the wet cobblestones. The camera approaches an ornate gazebo bandstand surrounded by stone balustrades and autumn trees, with historic buildings and warm street lamps visible in the background.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous first-person journey from a sheltered, lamp-lit colonnade into a wide, rain-slicked town square. It begins on a dark street lined with stone walls and iron fences, moving beneath a covered walkway where warm gas lamps illuminate massive stone pillars and glowing windows. As the camera progresses through the colonnade, the environment shifts toward a brightly lit theater entrance characterized by framed posters and the inviting glow of marquee lights. The scene then opens into a spacious cobblestone square dominated by the ornate Théâtre Râleur and the movement of horse riders and pedestrians under the night sky. Finally, as the camera pushes deeper into the plaza, rain begins to fall, transforming the ground into a glistening mirror that reflects the surrounding street lamps and an ornate gazebo bandstand.
